Gmail message ID: 19f213b150254457
Thread ID: 19f213b150254457

Email body:
​

Hello Team,

I came across DRS Engineering while researching innovative manufacturing, industrial equipment, automation, and engineering companies in the UAE.

I was curious to understand how your team currently approaches engineering validation and simulation activities during product development and equipment design.

When requirements such as structural analysis, design optimization, fatigue assessment, performance evaluation, or product validation arise, are these activities typically handled entirely in-house or do you engage external engineering partners when additional capacity or specialized expertise is needed?

At InternGrad Solutions, we support engineering teams with project-based Engineering Simulation & Product Validation services, including:

• Structural FEA

• CFD Analysis

• Thermal Simulation

• Design Optimization

using ANSYS, Abaqus, and SolidWorks Simulation.

I'd be interested in learning more about your engineering workflow and current priorities.

Looking forward to connecting.

Best Regards  

Arun Krishnan R A

Product Validation Services

notes:

Task area: DRS
From: Kelly Muir <kelly@litchfield.law>
Date: 2026-06-29 12:48
Subject: RE: Potential Forensic Retention | One Beverly Hills
Gmail message ID: 19f14ed8757bce8c
Thread ID: 19ee0c22ab58696c

Email body:
Dr. Salter,

Thank you again for talking just now. I know you will be traveling the next couple of days, so thank you in advance for trying to get the following over to us prior to your departure, if reasonable.

As discussed on our call, you received a call roughly six months ago from Geotility, a geothermal subcontractor on the One Beverly Hills project, asking you to opine on the safety of drilling foundational geothermal holes underneath shoring that had failed. You subsequently reviewed some LFA plans and wrote a letter to Geotility, advising them to not commence such work for safety reasons. Would you please provide a copy of that letter for our review?

In addition, if you could also provide your rate sheet, CV, and a retention agreement if you have one, we will get you approved and on board.

Thanks very much,
Kelly

title: Re:  california contractors
notes:
Source account: DRS <drs@drs-engineering.net>
Task area: DRS
From: Jordan Jurow <jordanj@inclineinsurancenow.info>
Date: 2026-06-30 05:20
Subject: Re:  california contractors
Gmail message ID: 19f187934a2cfe76
Thread ID: 19eb75152b31844b

Email body:
Dave,

Most brokers never actually run the class-code math. When we review a new contractor's dec page we keep finding the same thing: crews coded into higher-rate classes, 30 to 50 percent over.

That is why DL Falk and Precision Concrete moved their work comp to us.

Should an agent check DRS Engineering?

Thanks,
Jordan Jurow
Founder | Incline Insurance

On Thu, Jun 11, 2026 11:33:40 AM Jordan Jurow wrote:

> Dave,
>
> I am Jordan, founder of Incline Insurance. We have been the broker on the work comp and GL side for licensed contractors across California for seven years. DL Falk, Precision Concrete, and CertaPro Painters are on the book.
>
> California's work comp audit cycle has tightened up. We are seeing operators get caught with bigger payroll true-up bills than expected, and crews coded into higher-rate classes than they should be (sometimes 30 to 50 percent overpayment).
>
> Most of our contractor clients save 20 to 30 percent on the same coverage. Worth 10 minutes with me or one of the team?
>
> Regards
> Jordan Jurow
> Founder | Incline Insurance

title: Fwd: La Maddalena full day private tour 11.09 - 2 guests
notes:
Source account: DRS <drs@drs-engineering.net>
Task area: Personal
From: Robbie Kaye <robbiekaye@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-06-29 05:35
Subject: Fwd: La Maddalena full day private tour 11.09 - 2 guests
Gmail message ID: 19f1361483108d3e
Thread ID: 19f1361483108d3e

Email body:
All Night Long Songs, BMI
www.allnightlongsongs.com
www.robbiekaye.com

IG: @robbiekaye

Do what you love. Rumi

Begin forwarded message:

From: info@natoursardinia.com
Date: June 29, 2026 at 2:21:49 AM PDT
To: robbiekaye@gmail.com
Subject: La Maddalena full day private tour 11.09 - 2 guests 
Reply-To: info@natoursardinia.com

﻿ Buongiorno Robbie, This is Marina from NatourSardinia, thank you very much for reaching out. We would be very happy to have you on board with us next September :-) What you described is actually very close to the kind of experience we most enjoy creating. We also love the quieter, less crowded spots, and during our tours we always do our best to take our guests to beautiful coves and beaches where they can truly relax, swim, and enjoy the scenery away from the bigger crowds. Our private snorkeling tours are operated on RIB boats, this type of boat allows us to get as close as possible to the coastline and to explore smaller coves, hidden inlets, and more sheltered swimming spots that larger boats cannot easily reach. And our skippers speak English. :) I'm happy to explain how our tour works:

Our Maddalena full-day boat tour departs from Palau at 9.30 am and lasts about 5 1/2 hours, in which we stop 3-4 times, giving you plenty of time to swim, snorkel and go to the beach!   We will explore all the main islands of the archipelago- Spargi (Cala Corsara, Cala Granara, Cala Soraia, Cala Connari), Budelli (Pink Beach), Santa Maria (Natural Swimming Pools), and the wild Razzoli. 
Your captain, who is also a certified snorkelling and tourist guide , will be delighted to enhance your tour with an abundance of historical and naturalistic insights.

The tour is done on   a (Rigid Inflatable Boat),   which we have chosen because of its stability and ability to go very close to the coast, so that our guests can do a bit of sightseeing and also explore the most hidden inlets of the archipelago. For your comfort, each boat has a canopy for shade, a fresh shower, and a fridge.

On board, you'll have all the necessary snorkelling equipment at your disposal,   including a wetsuit and a full-face mask, allowing for easy swimming and breathing.   Additionally, during the tour, we provide a delightful aperitif featuring a selection of local and seasonal products, accompanied by Vermentino white wine, water, some soda and some juices.   If you have any specific preferences or needs regarding food or beverages, please let us know, and we will be more than happy to accommodate them.
You have the option to choose between two boat models (please refer to the enclosed pictures for more details):

The   Capelli Tempest -   This model is 8.50 meters in length and comes fully equipped with a canopy for shade, a hand shower, a fridge, and a small cabin. Extras such as a Stand-Up Paddle (SUP), towels, and a bottle of prosecco  are included. The cost for a full-day private boat tour is 890 euros, plus the cost of gasoline. The gasoline cost is 220 euros, which must be paid separately in cash. 

Available on the 12th: The   Revenger 24   - Measuring 7.50 meters in length, this boat also features a canopy for shade, a hand shower, and a fridge. A full- day private boat tour with the Revenger 24 costs 550 euros, plus the cost of gasoline. The gasoline price is 160 euros and must be  paid separately in cash

We may also have some larger cabin boats available for private charters, such as sailing boats or motorboats. These can be very comfortable, but they naturally have a bit less freedom when it comes to moving away from the busier areas as easily as the RIBs do.

I hope this is the kind of experience you were looking for,  please feel free to write to us anytime if you have any questions or would like more information. We are also available on WhatsApp.

Looking forward to hearing from you, 

Marina 
 
+39 334 7614858
+39 339 4774472 
Natour Sardinia 
All the Best in One Tour

title: Re: 25115 EJ Types
notes:
Source account: DRS <drs@drs-engineering.net>
Task area: DRS
From: Jody Robinson <jody@drs-engineering.net>
Date: 2026-06-26 10:36
Subject: Re: 25115 EJ Types
Gmail message ID: 19f05016008236e0
Thread ID: 19f04e2be8f39658

Email body:
Wyatt,

Upon review, I have some questions and concerns noted below.

My research suggests the quickrete product is most commonly recommended for
slabs and driveways. It also may have the following disadvantages for
walls:

- wicks water
- lower chemical resistance than X-Foam
- typically not used for this type of isolation joint
- high compressive strength for this type of application

On the other hand, the X-foam:

- compresses more readily (lower compressive strength than the quickrete
strip)
- does not wick water
- longevity
- specifically intended for this type of isolation joint

Have you all used this on walls like this before?

Thanks
*Jody Robinson, PE*
*President & Principal*
Jody@DRS-Engineering.net
Office: (818) 402-3962 Ext. 1
Cell: (360) 721-4548

*Los Angeles, Santa Barbara, San Luis Obispo,*
*Vancouver, Grand Rapids*

  ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏   ͏ 

Hello, 

We're raising rate limits across the native Claude API. As of June 12, 2026, 
we increased Claude Sonnet and Claude Haiku rate limits at every usage tier. 
And starting June 23, 2026, we're simplifying usage tiers. Your organization is 
moving to a higher tier.

What's changing: 

* Sonnet and Haiku rate limits increased at every usage tier. 
* We've simplified to three usage tiers: Start, Build, and Scale. Your 
organization moves to a higher tier immediately. 
* No organization receives lower limits than today. 

These changes apply automatically to your account; no action required. 

With higher limits, you can process more data, run more parallel requests, 
and keep agentic workloads running with fewer interruptions.

You can view your tier and current limits in the Claude Console 
, and find 
details in therate limits documentation 
. 
If you need more capacity, you can request higher limits from the Claude 
Console, or contact sales aboutPriority Tier 

for committed capacity.

—The Claude Team

notes:

Thread ID: 19f0157738d2dad7

Email body:
Jody:

These two notes are going to add a lot cost to this shoring job.

The spec that you have is very narrow and is going to impact costs in the following ways:

1. Prep and blasting of materials
2. Shipping and material delivery to paint shop and out to field.
3. Paint material spec costs.
4. What other options do we have?

Per your sheet SH-1.0 Perm Shoring notes

PILE COATING: ALL SOLDIER PILES SHALL BE EPOXY COATED FULL LENGTH OF BEAM OR 21 FEET
WHICHEVER IS LESS WITH INTERZONE 954 MODIFIED EPOXY COATING WITH A MINIMUM
APPLIED THICKNESS OF 15-20 MILS, PER MANUFACTURES SPECIFICATIONS.

STEEL PLATE COATING: ALL STEEL PLATES SHALL BE EPOXY COATEDWITH INTERZONE 954 MODIFIED EPOXY
COATING WITH A MINIMUM APPLIED THICKNESS OF 15-20 MILS, PER MANUFACTURES
SPECIFICATIONS.

title: 4299 Mesa Drive
notes:
Source account: DRS <drs@drs-engineering.net>
Task area: DRS
From: Zak Cook <zakcook.projects@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-06-25 18:16
Subject: 4299 Mesa Drive
Gmail message ID: 19f017fbf3111e21
Thread ID: 19f017fbf3111e21

Email body:
Hi Dave and Jody,

I hope this finds you both well.

I have somehow been roped into project managing the build of the new home
at 4299 Mesa Vista Drive. As you might recall, the north slope of that
property represents the eastern portion of the hillside that we worked on
in 2024. The new home was designed around the soil nails, and since taking
over the project and bringing a new architect on board, we have made
additional modifications to reduce the likelihood of any interaction with
the nails or their 3' radius safety buffer.

There is however, one area of the yard where we are struggling a bit and I
was hoping to get more information and insight from you. I have attached a
few pages of diagrams that illustrate the area in question at the northwest
corner of the property. Please note, we are showing the nails with their
safety buffer - as 6' diameter cylinders, as mapped by Jon Irvine's field
geologist. According to Jon, these locations are only rough
approximations. (We could, if necessary, have the critical nails surveyed.)

The critical things we are trying to understand are:
1) what are the ramifications of potentially intruding into the safety
buffer of a few of the nails?
2) what are the ramifications of actually encountering the grouted nail
shaft? Can this be remediated or reinforced in some way?

Would it be possible to schedule a short zoom call with one of you to show
you the model of the site and discuss the area in question? I would of
course assume this to be billable time for you.

Please let me know. We are trying to get the plans back to the engineers
by the middle of next week so your guidance would be most appreciated.

Best,

Zak

zakcook.projects@gmail.com
mobile: 310.963.4844

notes:

Flying to Sardinia via ny
notes:
Source account: DRS <drs@drs-engineering.net>
Task area: Personal
From: Robbie Kaye <robbiekaye@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-06-25 05:08
Subject: Flying to Sardinia via ny
Gmail message ID: 19efeaec35a023ee
Thread ID: 19efeaec35a023ee

Email body:
JetBlue uses dynamic certificate pricing for transatlantic flights. Depending on how busy the flight is, upgrading a JFK-to-Paris flight can cost anywhere from  1 to 4 certificates per person  each way. Since you are Mosaic 3, you have  4 certificates total . To successfully fly both of you in Mint business class on the way to Europe, your goal is to locate dates that require exactly  2 certificates per person . [ 1 ,  2 ]

📅 The Golden Windows in September
The optimal travel windows capitalize on the post-Labor Day drop-off in tourism when point costs drop, and Mint certificate requirements ease. [ 1 ,  2 ]
- Option A: Early-Mid September (Best Overall Balance) - Departure Window:  Monday, September 7 to Wednesday, September 9. 
- Return Window:  Tuesday, September 15 to Thursday, September 17. 
- Why it works:  Flying out right after Labor Day Monday captures a massive drop in leisure traffic, unlocking the lower 2-certificate-per-person threshold on the overnight leg to Paris.  [ 1 ,  2 ]

- Option B: Late September (Cheapest Point Costs) - Departure Window:  Monday, September 21 to Wednesday, September 23. 
- Return Window:  Monday, September 28 to Wednesday, September 30. 
- Why it works:  Historically, the last two weeks of September offer the lowest baseline economy point rates of the month. Lower cash/point fares directly correlate with a higher probability of finding 2-certificate Mint availability.  [ 1 ,  2 ]

✈️ Avoid These Peak Travel Days
- Avoid Thursday–Sunday Flights:  Transatlantic leisure travel spikes sharply over long weekends. These dates will rapidly drain your point balances and are highly likely to demand 3 or 4 certificates per person, which would exceed your account balance.  [ 1 ,  2 ]
- Avoid Early September Labor Day Traffic:  Do not fly between September 3 and September 6. Flights out of JFK to Europe are packed with holiday travelers, locking out upgrade space. 

🛠️ Step-by-Step Mosaic 3 Booking Strategy
Do not book anything online until you perform a quick availability check. [ 1 ]
- Check Open Upgrade Space:  Before redeeming any points, log into the  JetBlue Move to Mint Finder via Seats.aero  or log into your account on the  JetBlue App . Search the JFK to CDG route for your target September mid-week dates.  [ 1 ,  2 ,  3 ,  4 ]
- Confirm the Certificate Cost:  Look at the specific flight details. Ensure the display explicitly states it requires  2 certificates  to upgrade from economy to Mint.  [ 1 ]
- Call the Dedicated Line:  If you see 2-certificate availability, bypass the website glitches by calling your exclusive  Mosaic 3 Priority Phone Line . Have the phone agent book your baseline economy ticket using your 300,000 points, and ask them to immediately apply your 4 certificates to secure your Mint Suites right over the phone. 

All Night Long Songs, BMI
www.allnightlongsongs.com
www.robbiekaye.com

IG: @robbiekaye

Do what you love. Rumi
